titleStmt.title: SII 23.68: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 68. (A.R. No. 68 of 1907.) Tirumalai, Polur Taluk, North Arcot District Śikhāmaṇinātha temple—on nine detached stones lying in the courtyard

summary.p: These are disconnected fragments which might have formed part of one inscription, dated in the 23rd year and 175th day of some king whose name is lost. The object of the record seems to be the grant of exemption from taxes on a land belonging to the Jaina temple at Tirumalai in consideration of a lump-sum payment of money. The names are given of several persons who were probably officers of the tax department. The designations of two of these viz. Brahmārāyan Jananāthanār alias Rājēndraśōḻa-Brahmā[rāyaṉ] and Rājēndraśōḻa-Māvali-Vā[ṇādarāyaṉ?] seem to indicate that the king should have been Rājēndra-Chōḷa I.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
kuṭuppatākavum Āka Ivvūrkke yāṇṭu Irupattiraṇṭāvatu mutal vev veṟu—amutu vaikāvūr tirumalaimel
tūruṭaiyār poṟkāṭaṉ maturān=takanārum kṣatriyacikāmaṇivaḷanāṭṭu marukalnāṭṭuc coḻakeraḷa na tevaṉāccapiṭāranāruṅ kaḷat tūruṭaiyār tum
ttamur Apaṇṭampākkiḻāṉ Aṭikaḷ kāḷiyumiruntu yāṇṭu 2 10 3Āvatu nāḷ nūṟṟeḻupattaiñciṉāl variyiliṭṭatu Ivai puravuvari tiṇai

ṇa kalamuṭaiyāṉ nāṟpatiṉ kaḻañcu kka
nārkku brahmārāyaṉ jananātaṉārāṉa rājendraracoḻabrahma
murkiḻāṉ putta nāḻiyum varippottakanāyakam Uyyak ko

nāṟkaḻañce muṉṟu mañcāṭiyum nāṉmāvum
ppeṟūr nāṭṭup peṟūr neṟkuṉṟaṅkiḻā
triyacikāmaṇivaḷanāṭṭu tirunaṟaiyūrnāṭṭu maturāntaka nallūrkiḻ

jantracoḻamāvaliva ṉum
ṉāta vaḷanāṭṭu kān=tāranāṭṭu kkoṉūru kāl
yūr kkoṭṭattup pākūrnāṭṭu Ukkaluṭaiyāṉ paṉṟu kāṭaṉ cūṟṟiyum Ūṟṟukāṭṭukoṭṭattu nirveḷūrnāṭṭu coḻativākaranallūrt tā
Digital edition of SII 23.68 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
